beats.narration_text: Olá. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of mandelbrot set hidden inside newton's method. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of mandelbrot set hidden inside newton's method
output: ✓ Mandelbrot Set Hidden Inside Newton's Method

beats.narration_text: Mandelbrot Set Hidden Inside Newton's Method is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Newton iteration on p(z) = (z+1)(z−1)(z−λ) reveals Mandelbrot structure in λ-parameter space. Click left canvas to inspect dynamical basins.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Mandelbrot Set Hidden Inside Newton's Method

beats.narration_text: Newton iteration on p(z) = (z+1)(z−1)(z−λ) reveals Mandelbrot structure in λ-parameter space. Click left canvas to inspect dynamical basins.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of mandelbrot set hidden inside newton's method. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Mandelbrot Set Hidden Inside Newton's Method simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
